header.title: Safe Space
header.quickExitAria: Quick exit — leave the page immediately

disclaimer.title: Before we start talking
disclaimer.notMedical: This is not medical or psychological advice.
disclaimer.intro: I'm a space for conversation and emotional support — I don't replace a therapist, doctor or emergency services.
disclaimer.needHelpNow: If you need help right now
disclaimer.privacyNote: Your conversation lives only in this browser tab. Once you close it or clear data — it's gone for good.
disclaimer.consent: I understand and accept the terms above. I know that in a life-threatening situation I will call the emergency number {{emergency}}.
disclaimer.accept: I agree — enter the safe space

chat.greeting: Hi. I'm here to listen. There's no rush — write however feels comfortable. What brought you here today?
chat.footerNote: Your conversation stays only in this tab. Close it = it disappears.
chat.clearedDesc: All messages have been wiped from this tab.
chat.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
chat.exportHint: Download a copy of what appears in this tab. The file stays on your device.
chat.exportPrivacy: We do not store this file. You save it only on your device.
chat.exportFooter: This export is for your records only. It is not medical or psychological advice. If you are in danger, contact emergency services.

starters: Hi. I'm here to listen. What brought you here today?
Welcome. There's no rush and no need to choose the right words — where would you like to start?
Hey. How are you really feeling today — not the polite „I'm fine” version?
Hi. What's sitting with you most strongly today?
Welcome to a safe space. What would you like to get off your chest today?
Hey. If you had to name what you're feeling right now in one word, what word would it be?
Hi. What brought you here today specifically — and not on another day?
Welcome. Is there something you haven't had anyone to tell in a long time?
Hey. What's been going on with you lately — inside, not on the surface?
Hi. Tell me what kind of day you're having — even if it's hard to describe.
Welcome. What would you like someone to hear from you today?
Hey. Is there something weighing on you that won't leave you alone?
Hi. Where do you want to start — with what you feel, or with what happened?
Welcome. Is your head quiet right now, or very loud?
Hey. What was the hardest moment of your day?
Hi. Is there a thought that keeps coming back to you more often than others?
Welcome. What would you like to understand about yourself, if there were room for it today?
Hey. What are you arriving with — a specific situation, or a foggy feeling?
Hi. Has anything surprised you lately — about yourself or someone else?
Welcome. What would be the best possible outcome of our conversation for you?
Hey. How is your body responding to today — where do you feel tension?
Hi. Is there something you're afraid to say out loud?
Welcome. What was a small bright spot today, even if everything else was heavy?
Hey. What's been draining the most energy from you lately?
Hi. If your feelings could say something to you, what would it be?
Welcome. Is there a topic you've been avoiding that you'd like to touch today?
Hey. What in you needs the most attention right now?
Hi. Tell me about one thing happening in your life that you'd like to think out loud about.
Welcome. What are you missing the most today?
Hey. What happens inside you when you're left alone with yourself?
Hi. If you could feel anything other than what you feel right now, what would it be?
Welcome. What would you like your head to finally let go of?
Hey. What have you been telling yourself most often lately — and would you like that to change?
Hi. What was the moment today when you thought „I've had enough”?
Welcome. Is there a relationship that's occupying your mind today?
Hey. What would help you feel a little less alone in this right now?

replies: I hear you. What you're describing sounds really heavy. Would you like to tell me more about what you're feeling right now?
Thank you for telling me this. You are safe here. What weighs on you the most today?
I understand. Sometimes just naming what's inside takes enormous courage. What do you feel in your body right now?
I'm here with you in this conversation. Remember — if thoughts of harming yourself appear, you can call your local emergency number right away.
mock.crisis: What you're writing matters deeply to me. Your life matters. Please call {{emergency}} or {{trustline}} (helpline) right now. You can also press the “Quick exit” button if you need to leave this page.
